name: Prosperity Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank operating 280+ locations across Texas and Oklahoma, offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and wealth management services.
description_long:
Prosperity Bank is a regional FDIC-insured bank with a significant physical footprint of over 280 locations across Texas and Oklahoma. The bank has expanded through acquisitions, welcoming former Texas Partners Bank and American Bank customers to its platform. As a full-service financial institution, Prosperity Bank serves both consumer and commercial markets with comprehensive banking products.

The bank offers an extensive range of personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Simply Free Checking, eChecking, Royal Checking, Teen Checking), savings accounts with tiered interest rates, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, individual retirement accounts, and personal loans. Their mortgage division provides home purchase financing, refinancing, construction loans, home equity loans, and specialized programs like HOPP and HOPP/ITIN loans. On the business side, they provide commercial loans, SBA loans, lines of credit, equipment financing, and merchant services.

Prosperity Bank distinguishes itself through its extensive branch network—rare among modern banks—and specialized lending programs targeting physicians and medical professionals. The bank emphasizes digital accessibility, offering online banking, mobile apps with mobile deposit, Zelle transfers, Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and telephone banking through FastLine. Their Wealth Management division includes Prosperity Trust and ProsperityPrivate Investment services, indicating focus on higher-net-worth clients.

The bank serves a regional market effectively but lacks the national presence of larger institutions. While FDIC insurance and the branch network provide security and personal service, customers in non-service areas cannot access physical locations. The website emphasizes digital tools, suggesting the bank is modernizing, though regional banks may lag larger competitors in fintech innovation.
services: Personal checking accounts (Simply Free, eChecking, Personal, Royal, Interest Checking Plus, Teen)
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts with tiered interest rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Personal auto loans and personal loans
Residential mortgages, refinancing, construction loans, and home equity loans
Business checking and savings accounts with analysis features
Commercial loans, SBA loans, and business lines of credit
Wealth management through Prosperity Trust and ProsperityPrivate Investment
Digital banking including online banking, mobile app, and mobile deposit
Payment services including Zelle, Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and wire transfers
Merchant services and treasury management for businesses
Medical professional lending programs and physician banking services
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
280+ physical branch locations across Texas and Oklahoma for in-person service
Multiple checking account options including interest-bearing and teen accounts
Comprehensive mortgage services including FHA, construction, and ITIN loans
Specialized medical professional banking division with practice loans
Digital banking tools including mobile deposit, Zelle, Apple Pay, and Samsung Pay
Wealth management services through Prosperity Trust and ProsperityPrivate divisions
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Texas and Oklahoma only
As regional bank, likely has higher fees and lower rates than national competitors
Website provides minimal information about specific APRs, fees, or account minimums
No mention of online account opening process or ease of digital enrollment
Limited transparency on loan approval criteria and eligibility requirements

contact.address: 1301 N Mechanic St, El Campo, TX 77437

best_for: Texas and Oklahoma residents seeking full-service banking with local branch access
Medical professionals and physicians needing specialized lending and banking services
Business owners requiring commercial loans, SBA financing, and merchant services
Customers who prefer in-person banking relationships over purely digital banks
diagnosis: Prosperity Bank is best for Texas and Oklahoma residents who value in-person banking relationships, comprehensive financial services, and specialized lending programs—particularly medical professionals. The main caveat is geographic limitation: the bank only serves two states, making it inaccessible for customers outside these regions, and regional banks typically offer less competitive rates and higher fees than national or online-only competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 1301 N Mechanic St, El Campo, TX 77437
